tldr.text: Google's Gemma 4 12B lands on consumer laptops with native audio, vision, and agentic chops — no encoder required.
Menlo Ventures closes a $3B fund — its largest ever — riding a $14B stake in Anthropic after a bet-the-firm Series D in 2024.
The US government's export ban on Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models is reshaping how frontier AI gets deployed globally.

categories.name: Tools & Products
categories.blurb: From laptop-ready open models to personal finance dashboards in chat, the agentic product wave keeps crashing.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B runs on your laptop
stories.summary: Google released Gemma 4 12B, its first mid-sized open model with native audio and vision inputs and no multimodal encoders — just a clean LLM backbone. It fits in 16GB of VRAM, benchmarks near the larger 26B MoE model, and ships under Apache 2.0. Developers can run it locally via Hugging Face Transformers, llama.cpp, or MLX from day one.

stories.title: ChatGPT gets a personal finance dashboard
stories.summary: OpenAI is rolling out a personal finance experience to Plus users in the US, letting them securely connect bank and investment accounts and ask ChatGPT questions grounded in their actual financial data. The same release bumps GPT-5.5 Instant to default status for all users and ships ChatGPT for Excel and Google Sheets globally. The spreadsheet integration lives in a sidebar and handles formulas, budgets, and multi-tab cleanup in place.

stories.title: Google's Gemini Omni can create video from anything
stories.summary: Google's May recap confirms Gemini Omni as its new any-in, video-out model — combine images, audio, video, and text as input and get high-quality video grounded in real-world knowledge back out. Alongside it, Gemini 3.5 Flash is live for agentic coding and long-horizon tasks, with the Pro tier expected before end of June. The Googlebook laptop line from Acer, Asus, Dell, HP, and Lenovo is built around these models.

stories.title: OpenAI's GPT-Rosalind goes enterprise for drug discovery
stories.summary: OpenAI updated GPT-Rosalind, its life-sciences model, with GPT-5.5's agentic coding layered on top of stronger medicinal chemistry and genomics intelligence. The company also unveiled LifeSciBench, an externally expert-judged benchmark covering six workflow areas from evidence handling to wet-lab troubleshooting. Access is opening in research preview to eligible organizations globally via a trusted-access structure.

stories.title: Gemini comes to Google Classroom
stories.summary: Google unveiled a new Classroom app inside Gemini that lets teachers pull real class context into AI-assisted task planning securely. Students get Study Notebooks — personalized, adaptive lessons and quizzes — rolling out now to personal accounts. Chromebooks are also gaining Guided Learning tools designed to cut digital distractions during assignments.

categories.name: Startups & Money
categories.blurb: A week of milestone rounds — from India's newest AI unicorn to a VC firm turned Anthropic superfan.

stories.title: Menlo Ventures closes $3B on the back of Anthropic
stories.summary: Menlo Ventures announced $3 billion in new funds — its largest raise in 50 years — with its Anthropic stake now worth roughly $14 billion after leading the model maker's Series D at an $18B valuation in 2024. The firm's co-investment vehicle Anthology has also grown from $100M to nearly $250M deployed. It's a case study in concentrated conviction paying off spectacularly.

stories.title: Sarvam becomes India's newest AI unicorn
stories.summary: Indian AI startup Sarvam closed $234M of a targeted $300M Series B at a $1.5B valuation, with HCLTech contributing $150M as lead strategic investor. Bessemer, Khosla Ventures, and Peak XV also joined. The round follows Sarvam's launch of 30B and 105B open-source models and marks its leap from $41M raised across seed and Series A.

stories.title: Coralogix raises $200M to monitor the agent layer
stories.summary: Boston-based Coralogix pulled in $200M in a Series F — just 11 months after its $115M Series E — betting that the proliferation of autonomous AI agents will spike demand for observability tooling. The round, led by Advent and CPPIB, values the company at $1.6B post-money and brings total raised to $550M. The company argues agents fail silently in ways traditional monitoring tools can't catch.

stories.title: PhysicsX hits $2.4B valuation for manufacturing AI
stories.summary: London-based PhysicsX raised $300M in a Series C led by Singapore's Temasek, tripling its valuation to $2.4B. The startup builds AI models for designing complex manufactured components like jet engines and semiconductors, and counts Nvidia and Applied Materials among its backers. It's competing in a space that now includes Jeff Bezos-backed rivals.

stories.title: a16z backs compute marketplace Ornn
stories.summary: Andreessen Horowitz led a $33M seed round into Ornn, a marketplace where AI compute capacity can be bought and sold like a commodity. The a16z crypto arm participated alongside Galaxy Ventures and SV Angel — marking a16z's first bet on a compute trading venue. General partner Ali Yahya called it a foundational missing layer in the AI infrastructure stack.

categories.name: The Bigger Picture
categories.blurb: Export bans, state AGs, and courts wrestling with AI prompts as evidence — the regulatory tide is visibly turning.

stories.title: US bans Anthropic's top models abroad
stories.summary: The Trump administration issued an export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable access to its Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for foreign nationals, citing fears that users could exploit the systems to identify software vulnerabilities. The move caused a major service outage and triggered daily negotiations between Anthropic and US officials. G7 leaders are now discussing a trusted-partner framework that could restore access for allied nations.

stories.title: AI prompts are now discoverable in court
stories.summary: A federal magistrate judge in Conservation Law Foundation v. Shell Oil ordered plaintiffs to hand over the AI prompts their expert used to prepare her report, ruling the prompts are part of the expert's methodology — not protected drafting material. The decision is one of the clearest signals yet that AI-assisted legal work carries new disclosure obligations. Litigators are now rethinking how they document AI tool use in expert work product.

stories.title: Colorado reenacts and expands its AI law
stories.summary: Colorado quietly reenacted its landmark AI legislation in May 2026, replacing the original version with a sharper focus on adverse outcomes in high-stakes decisions — hiring, lending, housing — that use AI. Organizations doing business in Colorado must map their AI decision-making by January 1, 2027. The move adds to the growing state-level patchwork filling the vacuum left by stalled federal legislation.

stories.title: State AGs investigate OpenAI's safety practices
stories.summary: A coalition of state attorneys general has opened a joint investigation into OpenAI, seeking records on advertising, user engagement, data handling, and protections for minors and older adults. The inquiry adds to legal pressure from Florida's civil lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman over alleged concealment of ChatGPT safety risks. The xAI trade-secret lawsuit against OpenAI was separately dismissed with prejudice by a federal judge this week.

stories.title: German court holds Google liable for AI Overview errors
stories.summary: A German court ruled that Google can be held directly responsible for false statements appearing in AI Overviews, because the summaries constitute original content generated by Google rather than third-party search results. The ruling sets a significant precedent for how search AI answers are treated under media and defamation law across Europe. Google is expected to appeal.
